distribution_plot.caption: Distribution of 2 categories across 5,630 customers. Pie chart chosen for readability at this category count.

stacked_churn_plot.caption: Each bar spans 100% of customers inside that category. Red = churn rate. Orange dashed line = overall retention baseline — any category whose red segment extends PAST this line churns more than average (a hotspot).

chi_squared.narration: χ² = 350.93 on 1 df. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained.

cramers_v.narration: Cramér's V = 0.250 — moderate association with churn.

narrative.distribution: 'complain' has 2 category/ies across 5,630 customers. Most common: '0' (71.5%). Least common: '1' (28.5%).
narrative.churn_rates: Overall churn rate = 16.8%. Highest-churn category: '1' at 31.7% (1.88× the baseline). Lowest-churn category: '0' at 10.9% (0.65× the baseline). Hotspot categories (churn rate > 1.5× baseline): 1. Strong candidates for a binary flag feature in Stage 3.2.
narrative.association: χ² = 350.93 on 1 df. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained. Cramér's V = 0.250 — moderate association with churn.

recommendation.rationale: Moderate association (Cramér's V = 0.250). One-hot is sufficient; tree models will pick up the interactions.
engineering_ideas: Create binary flag(s) for hotspot categories: 1.
